title: Label the Magnet Poles
instructions_for_student: Drag the N and S labels to the correct ends of each magnet. Use the iron filing dot clusters as clues for where the poles are.

additionalProps.title: Label the Magnet Poles
additionalProps.subtitle: Chapter 4 – Exploring Magnets
additionalProps.instructionText: Drag the N and S labels to the correct ends of each magnet. Use the iron filing dot clusters as clues for where the poles are.

schema.description: JSON schema for the Magnet Pole Labelling Tool

title.description: Main heading shown in the header

subtitle.description: Chapter / subject tag shown above the title

instructionText.description: Student-facing instruction shown inside the header banner

schema.static_data_note: Magnet shapes (bar, U-shaped, ring) and pole slots are hardcoded in the component. The tool always shows all three magnets with 6 slots (2 per magnet). Only the text content is configurable via additionalProps.

quick_reference.purpose: Interactive drag-and-label tool for identifying North and South poles on three magnet shapes
key_features: Three inline-SVG magnet diagrams: bar magnet (horizontal rectangle), U-shaped magnet (horseshoe), ring magnet (concentric circles)
Iron filing dot clusters rendered as SVG ellipses at each pole region as visual hints
6 draggable N/S token pills — 2 per magnet
Touch drag: floating ghost element follows the finger; hit-tests slot rects on touchmove
Mouse drag: standard HTML5 drag-and-drop events
Correct placement locks the slot green with the label
Wrong placement shakes the slot and bounces the token back with a hint
Completion screen: key science fact + summary of all 3 magnet pole positions
Fully responsive grid — cards stack to single column on small screens

science_notes: N is always placed at the left/top for each magnet — this is conventional; agents should not change the slot ordering
Iron filing clusters appear at BOTH poles of every magnet regardless of shape
The completion screen reinforces the key NCERT concept: poles always exist in pairs
